concepts.label: Language
concepts.definition: A structured system of communication using symbols, sounds, and grammar rules to convey meaning.

concepts.label: Word
concepts.definition: The smallest unit of language that carries meaning and can stand alone.

concepts.label: Sentence
concepts.definition: A grammatically complete unit of expression consisting of a subject and predicate.

concepts.label: Grammar
concepts.definition: The set of structural rules governing the composition of words and sentences in a language.

concepts.label: Syntax
concepts.definition: The arrangement of words and phrases to create well-formed sentences.

concepts.label: Semantics
concepts.definition: The study of meaning in language — what words, phrases, and sentences refer to.

concepts.label: Pragmatics
concepts.definition: The study of how context influences the interpretation of meaning in communication.

concepts.label: Intent
concepts.definition: The purpose or goal behind a speaker's utterance — what they want to achieve.

concepts.label: Question
concepts.definition: A sentence type that requests information, marked by interrogative structure or intonation.

concepts.label: Statement
concepts.definition: A sentence type that asserts a fact or opinion, typically in declarative form.

concepts.label: Greeting
concepts.definition: A social speech act used to acknowledge another person's presence and initiate interaction.

concepts.label: Command
concepts.definition: A sentence type that directs someone to perform an action, using imperative mood.

concepts.label: Response
concepts.definition: A reply to a previous utterance, shaped by the intent and content of what was said.

concepts.label: Context
concepts.definition: The circumstances surrounding an utterance that affect its interpretation — who speaks, when, where, why.

concepts.label: Noun
concepts.definition: A word class that names a person, place, thing, or idea.

concepts.label: Verb
concepts.definition: A word class that expresses an action, state, or occurrence.

concepts.label: Adjective
concepts.definition: A word class that modifies a noun by describing a quality or attribute.

concepts.label: Subject
concepts.definition: The noun phrase in a sentence that performs or is described by the verb.

concepts.label: Predicate
concepts.definition: The part of a sentence that says something about the subject, containing the verb.

concepts.label: Meaning
concepts.definition: The information or concept that a word, phrase, or sentence conveys to a listener.

concepts.label: Reference
concepts.definition: The relationship between a linguistic expression and the thing it refers to in the world.

concepts.label: Ambiguity
concepts.definition: When a word or sentence has more than one possible meaning, requiring context to resolve.

concepts.label: Inference
concepts.definition: A conclusion drawn from evidence and reasoning rather than explicit statement.

concepts.label: Implication
concepts.definition: Something suggested by an utterance without being directly stated.

concepts.label: Metaphor
concepts.definition: A figure of speech where a word or phrase is applied to something it does not literally denote, to suggest a resemblance.

concepts.label: Conversation
concepts.definition: An interactive exchange of utterances between two or more participants following turn-taking rules.

concepts.label: Turn-Taking
concepts.definition: The mechanism by which participants in a conversation alternate between speaking and listening.

concepts.label: Speech Act
concepts.definition: An utterance viewed as an action — asking, promising, commanding, greeting — not just conveying information.

concepts.label: Discourse
concepts.definition: Connected speech or writing longer than a sentence, with coherent structure and purpose.

concepts.label: Phonology
concepts.definition: The study of sound patterns in language — how sounds combine and contrast to create meaning.

concepts.label: Morphology
concepts.definition: The study of the internal structure of words — how morphemes combine to form words.

concepts.label: Morpheme
concepts.definition: The smallest meaningful unit of language. Can be a whole word (free) or a prefix/suffix (bound).

concepts.label: Lexicon
concepts.definition: The complete set of words and their meanings in a language — the mental dictionary.

concepts.label: Synonym
concepts.definition: A word with the same or similar meaning as another word in the same language.

concepts.label: Antonym
concepts.definition: A word with the opposite meaning of another word.

concepts.label: Negation
concepts.definition: A grammatical operation that reverses the truth value of a proposition.

concepts.label: Quantifier
concepts.definition: A word that specifies quantity — all, some, none, every, many, few.

concepts.label: Proposition
concepts.definition: The meaning content of a sentence that can be true or false, independent of how it is expressed.

concepts.label: Predicate Logic
concepts.definition: A formal system for representing the logical structure of sentences using predicates, variables, and quantifiers.

concepts.label: Entailment
concepts.definition: A logical relationship where if sentence A is true, sentence B must also be true.

concepts.label: Coherence
concepts.definition: The property of a text or discourse where ideas connect logically and make sense together.

concepts.label: Anaphora
concepts.definition: Using a word (like a pronoun) to refer back to something mentioned earlier in discourse.

concepts.label: Deixis
concepts.definition: Words whose meaning depends on context — this, that, here, there, now, I, you.

concepts.label: Politeness
concepts.definition: Linguistic strategies used to maintain social harmony and respect the face of others.

concepts.label: Irony
concepts.definition: Expressing meaning by using language that normally signifies the opposite, relying on context for interpretation.

concepts.label: Topic
concepts.definition: What a sentence or discourse is about — the subject matter under discussion.

concepts.label: Focus
concepts.definition: The new or important information in a sentence that the speaker wants to highlight.

concepts.label: Presupposition
concepts.definition: Background information assumed to be true by the speaker when making an utterance.

concepts.label: Clause
concepts.definition: A grammatical unit containing a subject and predicate, which can be independent or dependent.

concepts.label: Phrase
concepts.definition: A group of words that functions as a single unit within a sentence but lacks subject-predicate structure.
